steps.title: Identify the punctuation boundary
steps.content: The comma after “amendment” signals the start of a non-restrictive modifier (“ratified in ______”) that must be properly closed. This opening punctuation pattern indicates that the phrase requires a matching closing comma before the main verb.

steps.title: Apply the boundary rule
steps.content:
The blank needs both the year and a closing comma to properly complete the non-restrictive participial phrase. Choice B (1804,) provides both: the year and the comma that closes the phrase before “separated” begins. The other choices either lack the closing comma, use incorrect punctuation marks, or omit the year.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
